task.title: Investigate LSP-backed context lookup for local review runner.

context.problem_statement: Definitions and usages can ground large-repo review better than raw file stuffing, but the local review runner needs a bounded LSP-backed context lookup design before any command or runner behavior is added.
background: AGW-032 added scripts/build_context_packet.py and make context-packet for changed-file adjacency using deterministic local git and text matching.
AGW-033 added a review-map Markdown/JSON artifact for large-changeset navigation and explicitly left LSP/codegraph lookup to AGW-034.
The source findings cite mrge.io for human-oriented code review UX with LSP context and logical file grouping, plus Sourcebot-style code search as a broader context-retrieval signal.

scripts/agent_review_run.py is currently a read-only review runner that loads startup context, personas, permission policy, executes manual or Amp review modes, writes artifacts, and fails on mutation.
The backlog delivery shape is Spike note, so this task should produce an evidence-backed recommendation and follow-on scope rather than shipping code.
non_goals: Do not implement an LSP client, codegraph indexer, semantic search service, or review-runner integration in AGW-034.
Do not add a command, Make target, schema installed into target repos, repo-contract-kit behavior, hosted service, or GitHub/bot integration.
Do not auto-install language servers, run package-manager installs, start background daemons, or contact remote code-indexing services.
Do not mutate scripts/agent_review_run.py, scripts/build_context_packet.py, prompts, generated adapters, or installed kit-managed files.
Do not mark the task done with a vague recommendation; the spike note must name the recommended architecture, refusal cases, test strategy, and follow-on backlog shape.
Do not use non-primary external claims when current official docs, source repositories, or local source evidence are available.

finalizer_receipt_paths: source main commit 5565519061b1c59e4518b649fb152553280cd341 completed AGW-033 and remote origin/main matched it
ignored local receipt directory .agent-workflows/tasks/agw-033-review-map-artifact/ recorded AGW-033 closeout; tracked worktree remained clean
previous_task_state.notes: AGW-034 packet creation began from a clean source checkout on main at 5565519061b1c59e4518b649fb152553280cd341. make agent-next selected AGW-034 with dirty false, make backlog-status reported 12 open and 87 done, and make agent-task-status TASK_STATUS_JSON=1 reported zero active tasks, zero hazards, zero stale tasks, zero unknown-scope tasks, and zero untracked agent worktrees.

closeout_required_before_start.reason: AGW-033 has been implemented, validated, pushed, and source-closed; source task-status reports no active, stale, hazardous, or unknown-scope tasks.
closeout_required_before_start.required_next_step: Proceed with exactly one AGW-034 worker in agent-workflow-kit; refresh git status and task-status before launch and stop if new dirt, active task metadata, or remote divergence appears.
evidence_paths: make agent-next selected AGW-034 with dirty false

goal_alignment.repo_goal: Keep agent-workflow-kit as the canonical source for local-first review workflow research, prompts, schemas, task packets, and generated adapters while leaving installed target-repo commands to repo-contract-kit.

area_contracts.purpose: New spike note that investigates whether and how LSP-backed definition/usage lookup should later compose with local review runner context.
Existing local read-only review runner; inspect to understand integration points but do not mutate in this spike.
Existing deterministic context packet; AGW-034 should identify how LSP lookup would compose with it, not replace it.
Existing AGW-033 review-map artifact; the spike should identify where LSP lookup evidence would appear in review maps.
Research matrix row for codebase context retrieval; closeout should update current state and remaining risk after the spike.

stop_conditions: Stop if implementation pressure turns the spike into runner, CLI, LSP client, or repo-contract-kit code.
Stop if bounded source research cannot be cited from local files or primary current sources.
Stop if the worker cannot define read-only, local-only, timeout, cache, unsupported-language, and omission behavior.
Stop if the recommended architecture would require background daemons, auto-installers, remote indexing, or account credentials.
Stop if the spike cannot clearly distinguish deterministic context packets, review maps, LSP lookup, and future codegraph indexing.

protected_files: repo-contract-kit managed templates or installed target-repo files
CHANGELOG.md unless the worker intentionally changes user-facing behavior, which this packet does not expect
unrelated backlog rows, unrelated task packets, and unrelated dirty work
expected_outputs: A spike note at research/agentic-workflow-review/spikes/agw-034-lsp-context-lookup.md with evidence, constraints, recommendation, architecture options, and follow-on task shape.
If the spikes directory is new, a short README explaining the purpose and closeout expectations for spike notes.
A recommendation of build/defer/no-go for LSP-backed lookup, with explicit rationale.

A proposed read-only local architecture for later work, including inputs, outputs, artifact schema sketch, runner composition points, timeouts, cache/sidecar boundaries, token caps, unsupported-language behavior, and omission fields.
A comparison of at least three approaches: pure context-packet/review-map fallback, local LSP definition/reference lookup, and heavier codegraph/search indexing.
A language/tool availability table covering at least Python, TypeScript/JavaScript, Go, Rust, and unknown/unsupported repos without requiring installation.
Security/privacy/resource analysis for local-only behavior, no auto-installs, no network indexing, no credentials, read-only filesystem expectations, timeouts, and generated/vendor exclusions.
A test and validation plan for any later implementation, including fixture repos or golden JSON artifacts.
Backlog, split backlog, summary, and feature matrix closeout after the spike is complete.

acceptance_criteria.description: A durable AGW-034 spike note exists and cites the local source surfaces plus any bounded primary external sources used for the investigation.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Inspect research/agentic-workflow-review/spikes/agw-034-lsp-context-lookup.md for local evidence references and source notes.

acceptance_criteria.description: The spike note gives a clear build/defer/no-go recommendation and explains how LSP lookup should compose with context packets, review maps, trace evidence, and the local read-only review runner.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Review the recommendation and architecture sections in the spike note.

acceptance_criteria.description: The spike defines later implementation boundaries: local-only, read-only, no auto-install, no remote indexing, bounded timeouts, explicit unsupported-language behavior, cache/sidecar policy, token caps, and omission reporting.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Review the safety, resource, and failure-mode sections.

acceptance_criteria.description: The spike compares pure deterministic context, local LSP lookup, and heavier codegraph/search indexing with concrete pros, cons, and go/no-go triggers.
The spike includes a later test plan and follow-on backlog shape instead of implementing runner behavior now.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Review validation and follow-on sections; confirm git diff contains no runner, prompt, schema, Makefile, or repo-contract-kit behavior changes.

acceptance_criteria.description: Backlog closeout records AGW-034 as a completed spike note and keeps any implementation work as follow-on scope rather than silently shipping it.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Run make backlog-check and make backlog-split-check, then inspect backlog rows and summary.

acceptance_criteria.description: Docs-impact and versioning checks pass with an explicit unchanged-version rationale if no behavior, prompt, schema, or user-facing command changed.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Run python3 scripts/check_doc_impact.py and VERSION_CHECK_ALLOW_UNCHANGED=1 make version-check.

closeout_requirements.dirty_state_explanation: Final handoff must say whether the checkout is clean, only expected files are dirty, cleanup is blocked, or unrelated dirt was preserved.

docs_impact.notes: This is research documentation and backlog closeout. It should not require VERSION or CHANGELOG changes unless the worker changes user-facing behavior, prompts, schemas, or commands.

known_risks: The task can expand from investigation into a half-built LSP client without enough language coverage or safety boundaries.
LSP servers can be slow, stateful, project-specific, or absent; a later design must treat missing servers as an omission, not a hard failure for every repo.
Auto-installing language servers or using remote code search would violate local-first and permission boundaries.
Definitions/usages can still be incomplete for dynamic languages, generated code, reflection, vendored dependencies, or monorepos.
A spike note can become stale if it does not record primary source dates and follow-on stop conditions.
stop_conditions: The worker needs to mutate runner code, prompts, schemas, Makefile targets, or repo-contract-kit files.
The worker cannot inspect enough local source or primary external sources to make a defensible recommendation.
The recommendation requires background daemons, auto-installs, remote services, credentials, or write access.
Validation reveals unrelated dirty work or generated adapter drift.

approval.notes: Active user goal is to add fully planned backlog packets and implement remaining workflow-stack items serially with one worker per backlog item.

token_budget.guidance: Keep the worker in a research-only slice. Use local source evidence first, then bounded primary external sources if internet access is available.

handoff.recommended_prompt: workflows/prompts/task-packet.md, then workflows/prompts/research/research-brief.md if the worker needs structured current-source research
dependencies: AGW-032 context packet exists and should be the deterministic fallback.
AGW-033 review map exists and should identify where LSP evidence would be surfaced.
AGW-014 review runner exists but must remain unchanged during the spike.
Any implementation backlog item should be explicit follow-on scope after the spike.
handoff.next_packet_hint: After AGW-034 closes, continue with the next open backlog item selected by make agent-next.
